title: Xtra Zesty Jamaican Chicken Skewers
description: A light and sour Jamaican dish featuring lemon and pressure-cooked chicken, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This Whole30-friendly recipe is a refreshing twist on traditional Jamaican cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, scotch bonnet peppers, garlic, ginger, thyme, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat a pressure cooker to high pressure.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and add it to the pressure cooker.
Close the lid and cook for 10 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 5 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Thread the cooked chicken onto the wooden skewers.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with additional thyme and lemon wedges if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
